title: Vilfa minima
taxonomy: Vilfa minima is classified as a species of grass within the genus Vilfa, which is a part of the family Poaceae. It is often classified as a synonym for the genus Stipa, which is a genus of grasses. The plant is native to parts of South America and is often found growing in dry, open areas. The classification of Vilfa minima is subject to change as new research emerges. Further studies are needed to confirm its exact classification within the plant kingdom.
uses: Vilfa minima is reported to have various medicinal uses, including being used to treat fever, and as a diuretic to relieve urinary problems. Its leaves are also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and as an antiseptic to treat wounds. The plant's roots are used to treat rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. In some cultures, it is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these uses.

intro: Vilfa minima, also known as Dwarf Vilfa, is a low-growing perennial plant that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Vilfa minima include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vilfa minima is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
